passed_law: License required to use a 3D printer to manufacture firearms; digital firearm manufacturing-code restrictions also apply.
active_proposal: AB 2047 active in Senate Appropriations; hearing date listed as August 3, 2026; would require firearm-blocking / blueprint-detection technology for 3D printers sold in California.
notes: Includes both an existing manufacture restriction and an active printer-level blocking proposal.

passed_law: HB 1144 signed May 4, 2026; prohibits 3D printing firearms/components except by licensed actors.
notes: No new direct printer-blocking proposal found in this sweep.

passed_law: Bans unlicensed 3D printing of guns and distribution of 3D-printing gun instructions.
active_proposal: HB 399 remains in House Judiciary from June 11, 2026; fiscal note marked complete; would require 3D printers sold or delivered in Delaware to include firearm-blocking technology if feasibility and rulemaking conditions are met.

passed_law: State law includes no unlicensed 3D printing of guns.
notes: SB 285 ghost-gun possession/transfer bill was not counted as active because LegiScan marked it introduced/dead.

passed_law: LD 1126 / Public Law 537 requires serial numbers on covered firearms/frames/receivers and prohibits undetectable firearms; expressly defines three-dimensional printers and printed unfinished frames/receivers.
notes: Not a printer-blocking mandate; added after new-state sweep.

passed_law: Regulates 3D printers and CNC milling machines for firearm manufacture; only qualifying licensed persons may use them to manufacture or assemble firearms.
notes: Restriction is narrower than a total printer ban.

passed_law: No current state 3D-printing firearm ban found.
active_proposal: SB 331/SB 332 passed Senate and remains pending House Government Operations; would ban unlicensed 3D printer/CNC firearm manufacturing.
notes: No new official movement found since June 25, 2025 referral.

passed_law: No current state 3D-printing firearm ban found.
active_proposal: HF 3407/SF 3661 would limit 3D printing of guns to federally licensed firearms manufacturers and prohibit distribution of 3D-printer firearm design files; no new official movement found in this sweep.
notes: Needs follow-up if Senate version is revived or reintroduced.

passed_law: Bans unlicensed 3D printing of guns; bans distribution/possession of 3D-printing instructions; bans plastic undetectable guns.
notes: AB 4975 enacted in 2026 strengthened digital-instruction restrictions.

passed_law: 2026 enacted budget bans using 3D printers to make ghost guns/parts and restricts digital instructions; includes a blocking-technology study and possible future mandate.
active_proposal: A2228 remains in Assembly committee for 3D-printer purchase background checks; enacted budget remains the key current-law item.
notes: Watch for working-group membership, standards, and implementation timeline.

passed_law: No current state 3D-printing firearm ban found.
active_proposal: HB 545 / S 409 would ban ghost guns and undetectable firearms; official summary says undetectable firearm includes manufacture through a 3D printing process.
notes: Not a printer-blocking mandate; added after new-state sweep.

passed_law: HB 40 enacted in 2026 restricts unserialized firearms/frames/receivers and defines manufacture to include additive processes and unfinished frames/receivers to include printing.
notes: Not a printer-blocking mandate; added after new-state sweep.

passed_law: HB 2320 signed Mar. 24, 2026; bans unlicensed 3D printing/CNC firearm manufacture and regulates digital gun blueprints.
active_proposal: HB 2321 official page still lists House Civil Rights & Judiciary, with no action since Jan. 12, 2026; would require blocking technologies in 3D printers.
notes: Media describes HB 2321 as dead/stalled in committee; official page still lists it in committee.
